articles.title: In a first, US Senate votes to halt Trump’s Iran war; President fumes, calls move 'meaningless'
articles.description: In a groundbreaking decision, the US Senate has passed a resolution that requires congressional consent for any further military action against Iran. This marks a pivotal moment in challenging President Trump's military strategy. Although the vote was close and a presidential veto is anticipated, the legislation reflects increasing anxiety regarding the rationale and economic repercussions of the ongoing conflict.

articles.title: Ayodhya Ram temple donation case: SIT recommends Trust revamp; findings may be placed before CM
articles.description: A Special Investigation Team has submitted its initial findings to the Uttar Pradesh government regarding allegations of embezzlement of donations for the Ayodhya Ram temple. The probe, initiated by the temple trust, is actively continuing as investigators gather more information. Officials confirmed the preliminary report was handed over to the Additional Chief Secretary (Home) by the Lucknow Divisional Commissioner, with further details expected as the investigation progresses.

articles.title: Cried out to papa for help: Cabbie spots 10-yr-old sleeping on pavement, abducts, rapes, kills her
articles.description: A father's fragmented memory of a white car and a yellow plate initiated a swift investigation. Investigators pieced together a digital trail, including CCTV footage and cab aggregator data, to track down Basu Kumar Singh, a driver accused of abducting, raping, and murdering a 10-year-old girl. The suspect was apprehended within seven hours of the initial report, later confessing to the heinous crime.

articles.title: 'Ye 12-15 saal khelenge': Goenka's old prediction on Rishabh Pant goes viral
articles.description: Sanjiv Goenka's bold 2025 prediction about Rishabh Pant's future with Lucknow Super Giants has resurfaced after the star wicketkeeper-batter's ₹15 crore trade back to Delhi Capitals. Goenka had envisioned Pant leading LSG to multiple IPL titles over 12-15 years, placing him alongside legends Dhoni and Rohit. However, Pant's two-season stint saw mixed results, culminating in this surprising player swap ahead of IPL 2027.

articles.title: "Charlie's shattered....": Charlie Kirk assassination case takes another twist as Candace Owens points to new SUV image evidence
articles.description: Candace Owens claims newly enhanced images of black shards inside the SUV that transported Charlie Kirk strengthen her theory that his RODE Wireless Pro microphone exploded. She said visible white markings on the fragments match the microphone's branding and cited a comparison with a destroyed RODE mic in a YouTube video. Owens argues the findings reinforce her theory, though investigators have not verified her claims or confirmed any link between the shards and the microphone.

articles.title: xQc suspended from Twitch after FIFA World Cup 2026 copyright claim explained
articles.description: Popular streamer xQc faced a temporary 48-hour suspension from Twitch after his channel received two copyright strikes. The action stemmed from a brief clip of FIFA World Cup 2026 match footage, reportedly around five seconds long and sourced from social media. This marks xQc's sixth suspension, highlighting ongoing debates about copyright enforcement on live-streaming platforms and the rapid dissemination of sports highlights.

articles.title: Gold, Silver Rate Today Live Updates: International gold, silver prices continue to drop as dollar strengthens
articles.full_text: Gold loans have become an increasingly significant component of retail lending over the past four years, with their share in retail loan portfolios more than doubling during the period. At the same time, finance companies have emerged as the fastest-growing category of lenders, steadily expanding their presence and capturing a larger share of the market, according to a report released by credit bureau Experian. The report, titled "Gold Loans in Transition", highlighted the strong momentum in the segment, noting that the share of gold loans in overall retail credit origination increased consistently from 18% in FY23 to 41% in FY26. The growth in gold-backed lending has coincided with a sharp rise in bullion prices. International gold prices, measured in US dollars per troy ounce, have surged about 130% over the past five years. Deutsche Bank has lowered its outlook for gold prices by slightly more than 20%, cautioning that the metal could decline to $3,800 per ounce if financial markets begin factoring in three to four interest rate increases by the US Federal Reserve. The bank said the balance of risks for bullion has shifted clearly to the downside due to changing monetary policy expectations. In a report on precious metals, Deutsche Bank analyst Michael Hsueh said the bank's revised central scenario projects gold at $4,800 per ounce in the fourth quarter, assuming the Federal Reserve keeps rates unchanged for an extended period. However, in a downside scenario where markets price in three to four rate hikes, gold could fall to $3,800 per ounce. The report noted that the repricing of Federal Reserve expectations, combined with continued strength in US economic data, has been the key factor driving gold prices lower. Data from Investing.com showed that August gold futures dropped 1.6% on Tuesday to $4,135 per troy ounce. The precious metal has retreated significantly from its record high of $5,589 per troy ounce, reached at a time when some market participants had expected prices to surpass the $6,000-per-ounce mark. Globally, gold prices have declined by nearly 10% over the past month. The report also highlighted weakening demand trends across Asian markets, signalling a deterioration in appetite for the traditional safe-haven asset. Dubai's commodities exchange plans to introduce a same-day settlement gold contract, its chief executive told Reuters. The initiative is aimed at attracting safe-haven demand and leveraging faster trading infrastructure to improve liquidity in the emirate's bullion market. Ghana's Gold Board announced that from July 1 it will align its gold pricing framework with internationally recognised LBMA benchmarks. The board will also impose strict limits on purchase prices as part of efforts to improve market discipline and reduce irregular trading practices. The US dollar climbed to its highest level in more than a year, making gold more expensive for holders of other currencies and reducing its appeal in international markets. US President Donald Trump said on Tuesday that Iran had agreed to allow nuclear inspections indefinitely. However, Tehran rejected that claim, stating that no such commitment had been made during the negotiations, raising fresh doubts over the durability of the fragile peace process. According to the CME FedWatch Tool, traders are currently pricing in three interest rate increases by the US Federal Reserve this year. Investors are awaiting the release of US Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) data on Thursday, the Federal Reserve's preferred measure of inflation, for additional clues regarding the future path of monetary policy. Gold prices extended their downward trend on Wednesday as growing expectations of additional US interest rate increases strengthened the dollar. Investors also continued to evaluate mixed messages emerging from the ongoing US-Iran peace negotiations. Spot gold fell 0.5% to $4,087.68 per ounce by 0116 GMT, touching its lowest level since June 11. US gold futures for August delivery dropped 1.1% to $4,105.40 per ounce.

articles.description: Devina Gahlot, daughter of BJP leader Kailash Gahlot, secured All India Rank 1 in CUET UG 2026 after the results were announced on June 23. In an interaction with ANI, she credited conceptual understanding over rote learning, stressed the importance of taking breaks during preparation, and said CUET largely tests NCERT-based knowledge through application-oriented questions.

articles.title: ICAI CA Intermediate May 2026 results to be out today: Check steps to download scorecards
articles.description: The Institute of Chartered Accountants of India (ICAI) is expected to announce the CA Intermediate May 2026 examination results by the evening of June 24. Candidates who appeared for the exams held from May 5 to May 15 can access their scorecards online using their roll and registration numbers. ICAI is also likely to release the merit list, toppers’ details, and overall pass percentage.

articles.title: India ranks 13th globally in QS World Future Skills Index 2027, emerges as a leading AI-ready economy
articles.description: India ranks 13th globally in the QS World Future Skills Index 2027, highlighting its strong readiness for an AI-driven economy. The country excels in Future of Work readiness, ranking fifth worldwide, supported by its vast digital workforce and economic growth. However, the report identifies significant skills gaps and graduate quality challenges that could hinder long-term competitiveness. QS recommends strengthening higher education, expanding industry-aligned skills development, and accelerating AI, digital, and green talent creation to sustain growth.

articles.title: 'People actually stop working': Indian woman shares the European habit she wishes India had
articles.description: An Indian expat in Germany, Himani Sharma, shares a profound cultural shift: Europe's ability to truly switch off after work. Unlike India, where work often intrudes on personal time, European evenings see shops close and people dedicate themselves to life beyond the office. Sharma highlights how this fosters deeper connections and present moments, a lesson she plans to carry forward to her next destination.

articles.title: 'I Will Find You': Britt Lower on Rachel-David finale romance
articles.description: Harlan Coben's 'I Will Find You' finale offers a touching, understated romantic moment between Rachel and David, stars Britt Lower and Sam Worthington. After a harrowing search for David's son, the couple shares a quiet hand-hold, signifying their shared journey and earned connection. Lower emphasizes the family's healing and their commitment to facing the future together, highlighting the show's hopeful yet realistic conclusion.

articles.title: 'Love and War' controversy: AICWA seeks FIR against SLB
articles.description: The tragic death of a worker on the set of Sanjay Leela Bhansali's 'Love and War' has ignited a firestorm of controversy. The All Indian Cine Workers Association is calling for an FIR against the director and demands a thorough investigation into the unsafe conditions. They also seek ₹1 crore in compensation for the victim's family and insist that filming is suspended until safety protocols are rigorously checked.
